blocks:
Sūrat al-Zumar

It is also called Sūrat al-Ghuraf, and it is entirely Makkan. *And it was said* that three verses, starting from قُلْ يا عِبادِيَ ** (Say, 'O My servants...') until their end, were revealed in Medina. And it was said that it is other than one verse. The verse قُلْ يا عِبادِيَ ** (Say, 'O My servants...') [is included]. Its number of verses is seventy-five verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) تَنْزِيلُ الْكِتابِ مِنَ اللهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ. (1) The sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise. (٢) إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ بِالْحَقِّ فَاعْبُدِ اللهَ مُخْلِصاً لَهُ الدِّينَ ** (2) Indeed We have sent down the Book to you with the truth. So worship Allah, dedicating the religion sincerely to Him. [Meaning:] free from polytheism (الشرك) and hypocrisy (الرّياء). (٣) أَلا لِلَّهِ الدِّينُ الْخالِصُ ** (3) Look! The exclusive religion belongs to Allah. because He is the One Who is unique in the attributes of Divinity (الالوهيّة) and the knowledge of secrets (الاسرار) and consciences (الضّمائر). وَالَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِهِ أَوْلِياءَ ما نَعْبُدُهُمْ إِلَّا لِيُقَرِّبُونا إِلَى اللهِ زُلْفى ** And those who take guardians besides Him [say]: 'We do not worship them except that they may bring us nearer to Allah.' by suppressing the saying [i.e., the saying is implied]. إِنَّ اللهَ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ فِي ما هُمْ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ** Indeed Allah will judge between them concerning that about which they differ. concerning matters of religion, so He will punish each according to his deserving. And it was said [it means] by admitting the truthful (المحقّ) to Paradise and the false (المبطل) to the Fire. And the pronoun (الضمير) refers to the disbelievers (الكفرة) and their opponents, or to them and their worshipped deities, for they hope for their intercession, while [the deities] curse them.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in a tradition: Then he صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم turned to the polytheists of the Arabs and said: "And you, why did you worship idols besides Allah?" They said: "We seek nearness to Allah, the Exalted, through that." He said: "Are they hearing, obedient to their Lord, and worshipping Him, so that you seek nearness to Allah by glorifying them?" They said: "No." He said: "Are you the ones who carve them with your own hands?" They said: "Yes." He said: "Then for them to worship you, if worship were permissible for them, would be more fitting than for you to worship them, since the One Who is aware of your interests (مصالحكم) and your consequences (عواقبكم), and the Wise One concerning what He obligates upon you, has not commanded you to glorify them."

In Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq, from his father (peace be upon them both): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, Allah, Blessed and Exalted, will bring forth on the Day of Resurrection everything that was worshipped besides Him, whether it be the sun, the moon, or anything else. Then He will ask every person about what he used to worship. The one who worshipped other than Him will say: 'Our Lord, we used to worship them so that they might bring us nearer to You زُلْفى ** (nearness).' He said: Then Allah, Blessed and Exalted, will say to the angels: 'Take them and what they used to worship to the Fire, except for those whom I have exempted, for those will be kept far away from it.'" إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي ** Indeed Allah does not guide He does not grant success (يوفّق) for guidance (الاهتداء) to the truth مَنْ هُوَ كاذِبٌ كَفَّارٌ anyone who is a liar, an ingrate, for these two are devoid of insight (البصيرة). لَوْ أَرادَ اللهُ أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ وَلَداً ** (4) Had Allah intended to take a son, as they claimed and attributed the angels, the Messiah (المسيح), and Ezra (عزيز) to Him. لَاصْطَفى ** He would have chosen لاختار مِمَّا يَخْلُقُ ما يَشاءُ from what He creates whatever He wished. It was said: meaning, He would not have taken a son according to their choice, such that they could attribute to Him whomever they wished. Rather, He would have chosen whomever He wished from His creation for that purpose. Its parallel is: Had We intended to take a diversion, We would have surely taken it from Ourselves. سُبْحانَهُ Immaculate is He from having a partner (الشريك), a consort (الصاحبة), or a son (الولد). هُوَ اللهُ الْواحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ He is Allah, the One, the All-subduing. He has no likeness (شبيه) among things, and He is not divisible in existence (وجود), nor in intellect (عقل), nor in imagination (وهم). Thus it is stated in al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), concerning the meaning of His Oneness (واحديّته), the Exalted.

(٥) خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ يُكَوِّرُ اللَّيْلَ عَلَى النَّهارِ وَيُكَوِّرُ النَّهارَ عَلَى اللَّيْلِ ** (5 He created the heavens and the earth with truth. He wraps the night around the day, and wraps the day around the night.) One of them covers the other, as if He wraps it around it, like the wrapping of clothing by the wearer, or He conceals it with it, just as the wrapped object is concealed by the wrapper (اللّفافة), or He makes it return upon it, a continuous returning (كروراً متتابعاً), like the succession of the coils (أكوار) of the العِمامة. وَسَخَّرَ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ يَجْرِي لِأَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى أَلا هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ** (and He subjected the sun and the moon, each running for a specified term. Behold! He is the All-mighty,) the Dominant over everything. ** الْغَفَّارُ ** (the All-forgiving) since He does not hasten the punishment.

(٦) خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ نَفْسٍ واحِدَةٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنْها زَوْجَها ** (He created you from a single soul, then He made its mate from it) Its interpretation has already preceded in Surat al-Nisa’ (Chapter of Women). وَأَنْزَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الْأَنْعامِ ثَمانِيَةَ أَزْواجٍ (and He sent down for you eight pairs of the cattle) Domesticated and wild [types] of cattle (البقر), sheep (الضّان), goats (المعز), and Bactrian (بخاتيّ) and Arabian (عراب) [types] of camels (الإبل), just as its explanation passed in Surat al-An'am (Chapter of Cattle).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "His sending down (انزاله) of that is His creation (خلقه) of it." يَخْلُقُكُمْ فِي بُطُونِ أُمَّهاتِكُمْ خَلْقاً مِنْ بَعْدِ خَلْقٍ (He creates you in the wombs of your mothers, creation after creation) A complete (سويّاً) living being (حيواناً) after bones covered with flesh, after bare bones, after a chewed lump of flesh (مضغة), after a clinging clot (علقة), after a sperm-drop (نطفة).

In Nahj al-Balaghah: "Or is it this [human] whom He originated in the darkness of the wombs and the folds of the curtains (شغف الأستار): a flowing sperm-drop (نطفة دهاقاً), a diminishing clot (علقة محاقاً), a fetus (جنيناً), a suckling infant (راضعاً), a newborn (وليداً), and a youth (يافعاً)." فِي ظُلُماتٍ ثَلاثٍ. (in three darknesses.)

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) and al-Qummi, he said: "The darkness of the abdomen (البطن), the darkness of the womb (الرّحم), and the darkness of the placenta (المشيمة)."

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), similar to it, and he added: "Where he has no means (حيلة) to seek nourishment, nor to repel harm (أذى), nor to attract benefit (استجلاب منفعة), nor to ward off injury (دفع مضرّة). Indeed, blood from the menses flows to him, which nourishes him, just as water nourishes the plant. That remains his nourishment until his creation is perfected, his body is strengthened, and his skin (أديمه) is strong enough to encounter the air, and his sight is strong enough to meet the light. Then labor (الطلق) is stirred up for his mother, disturbing her with the severest disturbance, and afflicting her until he is born." ذلِكُمُ اللهُ رَبُّكُمْ ** (That is Allah, your Lord) He whose actions these are, He is the One deserving of your worship and the Owner. لَهُ الْمُلْكُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ (to Him belongs the sovereignty; there is no god except Him.) since none other shares with Him in creation. فَأَنَّى تُصْرَفُونَ ** (So where are you being led away?) You are being turned away from His worship toward polytheism (الإشراك).

(٧) إِنْ تَكْفُرُوا فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَنِيٌّ عَنْكُمْ ** [Independent] of your faith (إيمانكم). وَلا يَرْضى لِعِبادِهِ الْكُفْرَ ** (7) If you are ungrateful, Allah is indeed Self-sufficient, [independent] of you, and He does not approve of ingratitude for His servants. Because of the harm it causes them, out of mercy for them. وَإِنْ تَشْكُرُوا يَرْضَهُ لَكُمْ But if you are grateful, He approves it for you. Because it is the cause of your salvation (فلاحكم). And it was recited with the sukūn (إسكان) of the hā (الهاء) and with the full vowelization (إشباع ضمّتها) of its ḍammah (ضمّتها). Al-Qummi said: This refers to the ingratitude for blessings (كفر النعم). And in al-Mahasin, narrated marfūʿan (مرفوعاً): He said: Al-Kufr (الكفر) here means opposition (الخلاف), and al-Shukr (الشكر) means al-Wilāyah (الولاية) and knowledge (المعرفة). وَلا تَزِرُ وازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرى ثُمَّ إِلى رَبِّكُمْ مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** No bearer shall bear another’s burden. Then to your Lord will be your return, and He will inform you concerning what you used to do. Through accountability (المحاسبة) and recompense (المجازاة). إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ Indeed, He knows best what is in the breasts. Thus, no hidden thing (خافية) regarding your deeds is concealed from Him.

(٨) وَإِذا مَسَّ الْإِنْسانَ ضُرٌّ دَعا رَبَّهُ مُنِيباً إِلَيْهِ ** (8) When distress befalls man, he supplicates his Lord, turning to Him penitently. due to the removal of that which contests the intellect in demonstrating that the origin (مبدأ) of everything is from Him, glory be to Him. ثُمَّ إِذا خَوَّلَهُ ** He gives him out of favor (تفضّلاً), for the granting (التحْويل) is specific to favor (التفضّل). نِعْمَةً مِنْهُ from Allah نَسِيَ ما كانَ يَدْعُوا إِلَيْهِ ** meaning, the distress (الضّرّ) which he was supplicating to Allah to remove مِنْ قَبْلُ ** from before the blessing. وَجَعَلَ لِلَّهِ أَنْداداً (and sets up rivals to Allah) شركاء (partners) لِيُضِلَّ عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ (that he may lead [people] astray from His way). And it was recited (وقرئ) with the opening (فتح) of the yā’ (i.e., لِيُضَلَّ - that he may be led astray). قُلْ تَمَتَّعْ بِكُفْرِكَ قَلِيلاً إِنَّكَ مِنْ أَصْحابِ النَّارِ Say, 'Enjoy your faithlessness for a little while; you will indeed be among the inmates of the Fire.' This is a command of threat, containing an indication that disbelief (الكفر) is a type of desire (تشهّى) that has no basis (مستند) and is a means of despairing the disbelievers from enjoying themselves in the Hereafter.

القمّيّ (said): It was revealed concerning Abū Fulān (so-and-so).

And in al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: It was revealed concerning Abū al-Fasīl. He used to consider the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم a sorcerer (ساحراً). So, when distress (الضرّ) afflicted him—meaning sickness (السّقم)—دَعا رَبَّهُ مُنِيباً إِلَيْهِ ** (he supplicates his Lord, turning to Him penitently), meaning: repenting to Him from what he used to say about the Messenger of Allah. ثُمَّ إِذا خَوَّلَهُ نِعْمَةً مِنْهُ ** (Then, when He grants him a blessing from Him), meaning: health (العافية). نَسِيَ ما كانَ يَدْعُوا إِلَيْهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (he forgets what he was supplicating for from before), meaning: he forgot the repentance (التّوبة) to Allah, the Exalted, from what he used to say about the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, that he was a sorcerer (ساحر). And for that reason, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: قُلْ تَمَتَّعْ بِكُفْرِكَ قَلِيلاً إِنَّكَ مِنْ أَصْحابِ النَّارِ ** (Say, 'Enjoy your faithlessness for a little while; you will indeed be among the inmates of the Fire'), meaning: your command (امْرتَكَ) over the people without right from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and from His Messenger. He said: Then the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, turned to ‘Alī (peace be upon him), informing of his state and his virtue (فضله) in the sight of Allah, the Blessed and Exalted. So He said:

(٩) أَمَّنْ هُوَ قانِتٌ آناءَ اللَّيْلِ ساجِداً وَقائِماً يَحْذَرُ الْآخِرَةَ وَيَرْجُوا رَحْمَةَ رَبِّهِ قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِي الَّذِينَ يَعْلَمُونَ (9) Is he who is obedient, prostrating and standing [in prayer] during the watches of the night, wary of the Hereafter and hopeful of the mercy of his Lord, [like the faithless one]? Say, ‘Are those who know equal to those who do not know?’ that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم وَالَّذِينَ لا يَعْلَمُونَ that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, or [who believe] that He is a lying sorcerer. إِنَّما يَتَذَكَّرُ أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ Then he said: "This is its interpretation (تأويله)."

And in it (al-Safi) and in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: prostrating and standing [in prayer] during the watches of the night, He said: "It means the night prayer (صلاة اللّيل)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "We are those who know, and our enemy is those who do not know, and our followers (شيعتنا) are the people of the intellect."

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Allah has indeed mentioned us, our followers (شيعتنا), and our enemy in a single verse of His Book, when He said: Say, ‘Are those who know equal...’ (the Ayah)." Then He interpreted it with what was mentioned.

And from al-Hasan al-Mujtaba (peace be upon him) and al-Qummi: the people of the intellect are the people of understanding (أولو العقول). And أَمَّنْ هُوَ was read with the lightening (تخفيف) of the mīm (as أَمَنْ هُوَ).

(١٠) قُلْ يا عِبادِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمْ ** (10) Say, 'O My servants who have faith! Be wary of your Lord.' by adhering to obedience to Him. لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا فِي هذِهِ الدُّنْيا حَسَنَةٌ ** For those who do good in this world there is a good reward. The circumstantial phrase (الظّرف) is either related to "do good" (أَحْسَنُوا) or to "a good reward" (حَسَنَةٌ). If related to the former, the good reward (الحسنة) encompasses the good reward of both abodes (الدارين). If related to the latter, it does not negate the attainment of the good reward of the Hereafter as well. The good reward (الحسنة) in this world is like health (الصحّة) and well-being (العافية).

In al-Amali, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Indeed, the believer works for three types of reward (الثواب). As for the good (لخير), Allah rewards him for his deed in his worldly life. Then he recited this Ayah. Then he said: So, those whom Allah grants in this world, He will not hold them accountable for it in the Hereafter. وَأَرْضُ اللهِ واسِعَةٌ ** And Allah's earth is vast. So, whoever finds it difficult to achieve abundance in doing good (الإحسان) in his homeland, let him emigrate to where he is able to do so. إِنَّما يُوَفَّى الصَّابِرُونَ ** The patient will indeed be paid their reward (The patient) regarding the hardships of obedience, such as enduring affliction (البلاء) and emigrating from homelands for its sake. أَجْرَهُمْ بِغَيْرِ حِسابٍ ** in full, without any reckoning. A reward (اجر) to which the calculation of the reckoners (حساب الحساب) cannot lead.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "When the registers (الدواوين) are spread out and the scales (الموازين) are set up, no scale will be set up for the people of affliction (أهل البلاء), nor will any register be spread out for them." Then he recited this Ayah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "When the Day of Resurrection comes, a group (عنق) of people will stand up and come to the gate of Paradise and knock on it. It will be said to them: 'Who are you?' They will say: 'We are the people of patience (أهل الصبر).' It will be said to them: 'Over what did you remain patient?' They will say: 'We were patient in obeying Allah and patient in refraining from disobeying Allah.' Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will say: 'They have spoken the truth. Admit them into Paradise.' And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنَّما يُوَفَّى الصَّابِرُونَ أَجْرَهُمْ بِغَيْرِ حِسابٍ. (The patient will indeed be paid their reward in full, without any reckoning.)"

(١١) قُلْ إِنِّي أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ اللهَ مُخْلِصاً لَهُ الدِّينَ ** (11) Say, 'I have been commanded to worship Allah, dedicating my religion sincerely to Him.' Unifying Him (in worship). (١٢) وَأُمِرْتُ لِأَنْ أَكُونَ أَوَّلَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ** (12) And I have been commanded to be the first of those who submit.' Their foremost one in this world and the Hereafter. (١٣) قُلْ إِنِّي أَخافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّي ** (13) Say, 'Indeed I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous day.' by abandoning sincerity عَذابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (١٤) قُلِ اللهَ أَعْبُدُ مُخْلِصاً لَهُ دِينِي ** (14) Say, 'I worship Allah, dedicating my religion sincerely to Him.' In compliance with His command. (١٥) فَاعْبُدُوا ما شِئْتُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ ** (15) 'So worship whatever you wish besides Him.' A threat and abandonment (خذلان) for them قُلْ إِنَّ الْخاسِرِينَ ** Say, 'Indeed the losers are those who lose themselves and their families.' Those who are complete in loss (الخسران) الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَأَهْلِيهِمْ. al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: They were cheated (غُبِنُوا) يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ أَلا ذلِكَ هُوَ الْخُسْرانُ الْمُبِينُ. on the Day of Resurrection. Look! That is the manifest loss.

(١٦) لَهُمْ مِنْ فَوْقِهِمْ ظُلَلٌ مِنَ النَّارِ ** (For them there will be) layers (اطباق) of it (the Fire) covering them. وَمِنْ تَحْتِهِمْ ظُلَلٌ ** coverings from above and layers (أطباق). It was said that these are the coverings (ظلل) of the others. ذلِكَ يُخَوِّفُ اللهُ بِهِ عِبادَهُ ** coverings from below. With that Allah frightens His servants. That torment is what He frightens them with, so that they may avoid what causes them to fall into it. يا عِبادِ فَاتَّقُونِ ** O My servants, be wary of Me! And do not expose yourselves to that which necessitates My wrath (سخطي).

(١٧) وَالَّذِينَ اجْتَنَبُوا الطَّاغُوتَ أَنْ يَعْبُدُوها وَأَنابُوا إِلَى اللهِ لَهُمُ الْبُشْرى ** (17) And those who shunned the Tāghūt from worshipping it, and turned penitently to Allah, for them is the good news.

** وَالَّذِينَ اجْتَنَبُوا الطَّاغُوتَ : The one who reached the limit of transgression (الطّغيان). أَنْ يَعْبُدُوها ** وَأَنابُوا إِلَى اللهِ : And they approached Him with their whole selves (بشراشرهم), turning away from everything besides Him. لَهُمُ الْبُشْرى : With the reward, upon the tongues of the Messengers and upon the tongues of the angels at the presence of death.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "You are them, and whoever obeys a tyrant (جَبَّارًا) has worshipped him. So give good news to My servants (فَبَشِّرْ عِبادِ)."

(18) ** الَّذِينَ يَسْتَمِعُونَ الْقَوْلَ فَيَتَّبِعُونَ أَحْسَنَهُ ** (those who listen to the word and follow the best of it) They distinguish between the truth (الحقّ) and the falsehood (الباطل) and prefer the best (الأفضل). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah gave glad tidings to the people of intellect (العقل) and understanding (الفهم) in His Book, and He said: فَبَشِّرْ (so give good news). And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He is the one who hears the narration (الحديث) and relates it exactly as he heard it, neither adding to it nor subtracting from it. And in a narration (رواية): They are those who submit (المسلّمون) to the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), who, when they hear the narration (الحديث), neither add to it nor subtract from it; they bring it exactly as they heard it. أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ هَداهُمُ اللهُ ** (They are the ones whom Allah has guided) to His religion. وَأُولئِكَ هُمْ أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ ** (and it is they who possess intellect) The sound intellects (العقول السّليمة) free from the contention (منازعة) of illusion (الوهم) and custom (العادة).

**(١٩) أَفَمَنْ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِ كَلِمَةُ الْعَذابِ أَفَأَنْتَ تُنْقِذُ مَنْ فِي النَّارِ ** (19) Is he on whom the sentence of punishment has become due—can you save someone who is in the Fire?

[This is] a denial and a deeming unlikely of the saving of him upon whom the sentence (الكلمة) of the Fire has become due, by striving in calling him to faith (الإِيمان). And [it is] an indication that whoever has been decreed to receive the punishment is like one who has already fallen into it, because the reversal (الخلف) of it is impossible.

** لكِنِ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا رَبَّهُمْ لَهُمْ غُرَفٌ مِنْ فَوْقِها غُرَفٌ ** (20) But those who are wary of their Lord, there are chambers for them, above which are [other] chambers, Some of them are high above others, built (مَبْنِيَّةٌ), constructed in the manner of houses on the earth, تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ (beneath which rivers flow). وَعْدَ اللهِ لا يُخْلِفُ اللهُ الْمِيعادَ. (This is) Allah’s promise. Allah does not break the promise.

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Ali asked the Messenger of Allah (may the blessings of Allah be upon both of them) concerning the interpretation (تفسير) of this Ayah: "With what were these chambers built, O Messenger of Allah?"

So He said: "O Ali (peace be upon him), those are chambers which Allah built for His friends (أوليائه) with pearls, rubies, and chrysolite. Their roofs are gold, interwoven (محبوكة) with silver. For every chamber among them, there are a thousand doors of gold, and at every door there is an angel entrusted (موكّل) with it. And in them are elevated couches, some above others, made of silk and brocade in different colors. And their stuffing is musk, ambergris, and camphor. And that is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: **وَفُرُشٍ مَرْفُوعَةٍ ** (and elevated couches)."

The narrative (الحديث). Some of it has preceded in Surat al-Fatir and some of it in Surat al-Ra'd.

(21) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ أَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَسَلَكَهُ يَنابِيعَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (21) Have you not regarded that Allah sends down water from the sky, and then makes it flow as springs in the earth? springs (عيوناً) and wells (ركايا). ثُمَّ يُخْرِجُ بِهِ زَرْعاً مُخْتَلِفاً أَلْوانُهُ ثُمَّ يَهِيجُ ** then He brings forth thereby crops of diverse hues. Then they wither, it rises (يثور) from its place of growth due to dryness, so you see it yellowed (مُصْفَرًّا) from its dryness. ثُمَّ يَجْعَلُهُ حُطاماً ** and you see them yellowed; then He turns them into chaff. fragments (فتاتاً). إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَذِكْرى ** There is indeed a reminder in that for the remembrance (لتذكير) of His signs (آياته), that there must be a Wise Maker (صانع حكيم) Who managed it and perfected it (دبّره وسوّاه), and that it is like the life of this world (الحياة الدنيا), so one should not be deceived by it (فلا يغترّ بها). **لِأُولِي الْأَلْبابِ ** for those who possess intellect. since none but they remember through it.

(٢٢) أَفَمَنْ شَرَحَ اللهُ صَدْرَهُ لِلْإِسْلامِ ** (Is he whose breast Allah has opened to Islam) until it became firmly established therein with ease فَهُوَ عَلى نُورٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِ.** (so he is upon a light from his Lord)

In Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny): He recited this Ayah and said: "Indeed, when the light falls upon the heart, it expands for it and becomes open." They said: "O Messenger of Allah, is there a sign by which this is known?" He said: "Detachment (التجافي) from the abode of delusion (دار الغرور), turning (الإنابة) towards the abode of eternity (دار الخلود), and preparing for death before its descent."

And al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), while the general populace (العامّة) say it was revealed concerning Hamzah and Ali. And what follows it was revealed concerning Abu Lahab and his son: فَوَيْلٌ لِلْقاسِيَةِ قُلُوبُهُمْ مِنْ ذِكْرِ اللهِ ** (Woe to those whose hearts are hardened against the remembrance of Allah!) —meaning, because of His remembrance (ذكره). And this hardening is more resistant (تأبّياً) to accepting Him than the one who is hardened due to another reason, so who is more eloquent here?

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Hardness (القسوة) and softness (الرقّة) originate from the heart, and that is His saying: فَوَيْلٌ ** [the rest of] the Ayah. أُولئِكَ فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ.** (They are in manifest error.)

(٢٣) اللهُ نَزَّلَ أَحْسَنَ الْحَدِيثِ ** (Allah has sent down the best of discourses) meaning the Quran. كِتاباً مُتَشابِهاً ** (a Book consistent [with itself]) resembling one another in miraculousness (الإِعجاز), the harmony of its composition (النّظم), the soundness of its meaning, and its indication of general benefits, as has been said. مَثانِيَ (reiterated) wherein the discourse is repeated (ثنيّ), recurring. This is how it was mentioned in one of the ways the Opening Chapter of the Book (فاتحة الكتاب) was named by it, and other meanings for it have passed in Surat al-Hijr. The singular (Book) was described by the plural (reiterated) because the Book is a whole (جملة) possessing details (تفاصيل). If مَثانِيَ is made a specification (تميزاً) for مُتَشابِهاً , the meaning would be: whose dispositions (تصاريفه) are consistent. It was said: The benefit in repetition (التكرير) and reiteration (التّثنية) is that souls are averse to advice (النصيحة) and admonitions (المواعظ). Thus, unless they are repeated to them, time after time, they will not become firmly established within them.

I say: And this is His saying, the Glorified: وَلَقَدْ ضَرَبْنا لِلنَّاسِ فِي هذَا الْقُرْآنِ مِنْ كُلِّ مَثَلٍ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ تَقْشَعِرُّ مِنْهُ جُلُودُ الَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ ** (Certainly, We have drawn all kinds of parables for the people in this Quran so that they may take admonition. The skins of those who fear their Lord shiver at [the recitation of] it) they contract and shrink back (تشمئزّ) out of fear of the threat (الوعيد) contained within it, and this is a parable for the intensity of fear.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "When the skin of a servant shivers out of the fear (خشية) of Allah, his sins fall away from him just as the leaves fall away from a dry tree." ثُمَّ تَلِينُ جُلُودُهُمْ وَقُلُوبُهُمْ إِلى ذِكْرِ اللهِ ** (Then their skins and their hearts soften at the remembrance of Allah.) they become tranquil toward Him through mercy and the universality of forgiveness (عموم المغفرة). ذلِكَ هُدَى اللهِ يَهْدِي بِهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللهُ ** (That is Allah’s guidance, with which He guides whomever He wishes. And whomever Allah leads astray,) and whomever He abandons (يخذله), فَما لَهُ مِنْ هادٍ (there is no guide for him) who can bring him out of error (الضلال).

(٢٤) أَفَمَنْ يَتَّقِي بِوَجْهِهِ ** (24) Is he who wards off with his face He makes it (his face) his shield, protecting himself with it, because his hands are shackled to his neck, so he is unable to ward off (the punishment) except with his face سُوءَ الْعَذابِ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** the worst of the punishment on the Day of Resurrection (Is he) like one who is safe from it? So the predicate (الخبر) was omitted, just as it was omitted in similar instances (نظائره). وَقِيلَ لِلظَّالِمِينَ ** and it is said to the wrongdoers: Meaning: to them. So the explicit noun (الظاهر) was placed in its stead (the pronoun) to register the wrongdoing (الظّلم) against them and to indicate the reason (الموجب) for what is said to them. ذُوقُوا ما كُنْتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ ** 'Taste what you used to earn!' Meaning: its consequence (وباله).

(25) كَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ فَأَتاهُمُ الْعَذابُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** (25) Those who were before them also denied, so the punishment overtook them from where they did not perceive. from the direction (الجهة) which did not occur to their minds that the evil (الشرّ) would come to them from it. (26) فَأَذاقَهُمُ اللهُ الْخِزْيَ ** (26) So Allah made them taste disgrace in the life of the world, but the punishment of the Hereafter is surely greater, if they but knew. the humiliation (الذلّ) فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** like transformation (المسخ), sinking (الخسف), killing (القتل), captivity (السّبي), and expulsion (الاجلاء). And the punishment of the Hereafter, prepared for them, is greater due to its severity and permanence لَوْ كانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ.** they would have taken heed by it and avoided it.

(٢٧) وَلَقَدْ ضَرَبْنا لِلنَّاسِ فِي هذَا الْقُرْآنِ مِنْ كُلِّ مَثَلٍ ** (27) Certainly We have drawn for the people in this Quran every [kind of] parable, which the observer needs concerning the matter of his religion, لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (that they may take admonition) by it. (٢٨) قُرْآناً عَرَبِيًّا غَيْرَ ذِي عِوَجٍ ** (28) an Arabic Quran, free of any crookedness, there being no defect (اختلال) in it whatsoever, لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ. (that they may be Godwary.) (٢٩) ضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً ** (29) Allah draws a parable: for the polytheist (المشرك) and the monotheist (الموحّد): رَجُلاً فِيهِ شُرَكاءُ مُتَشاكِسُونَ ** (a man owned by several quarrelsome partners,) disputing, differing, وَرَجُلاً سَلَماً لِرَجُلٍ ** (and a man belonging wholly to one man.) purely belonging to one, over whom no one else has any claim. And it was recited (سَالِماً).

It was said: This is a parable for the polytheist (المشرك), according to what his doctrine (مذهبه) necessitates, in that each of his deities (معبودية) claims his servitude (عبوديّته), and they dispute over him—a slave shared by a group who pull him in different directions and take turns assigning him to their various tasks, resulting in his confusion (تحيّره) and the distraction of his heart. And the monotheist (الموحّد) is likened to the one who belongs purely to one, over whom no one else has any claim.

And al-Qummi said: This is a parable drawn by Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and for his partners (شركائه) who wronged Him and usurped His right. His saying مُتَشاكِسُونَ ** means mutually hateful (متباغضون). And His saying وَرَجُلاً سَلَماً لِرَجُلٍ ** refers to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) being wholly devoted (سَلَم) to the Messenger of Allah (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both).

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: "Behold, I am specified in the Quran with names. Beware lest you be overcome by them and thus go astray in your religion. I am the wholly devoted one (السَّلَمُ) to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: وَرَجُلاً سَلَماً لِرَجُلٍ. (and a man belonging wholly to one man.)"

In al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): "I am that man, the wholly devoted one (السلم) to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله."

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The man belonging wholly to one man (الرجل السلم لرجل) is truly Ali and his followers (شيعته)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "As for the one فِيهِ شُرَكاءُ مُتَشاكِسُونَ ** (owned by several quarrelsome partners), it is the first 'so-and-so' (فلان الأوّل) whose guardianship (ولايته) is gathered by the scattered ones, and in doing so, some of them curse others, and some disavow others. And as for the man belonging wholly to one man (رجل سلم لرجل), it is truly the first 'so-and-so' (فلان الأوّل) and his followers (شيعته)."

I say: He (peace be upon him) meant by the first 'so-and-so' (فلان الأوّل) in the first instance of his saying, Abu Bakr, for he was the first of the caliphs falsely (باطلاً). And in the second instance of his saying, He meant the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), for He was the first of the caliphs truly (حقّاً). He only qualified the second instance with His saying "truly (حقّاً)" and did not qualify the first instance with His saying "falsely (باطلاً)" because the second instance required that contextual indicator (القرينة) for understanding the intended meaning, unlike the first, as is evident. Thus, the reason for the disagreement (تخالف) among the companions of Abu Bakr is that Abu Bakr was not wholly devoted (سلماً) to Allah and His Messenger, neither in the matter of the Imamate (الامارة) nor in the rulings (الأحكام) built upon it. And his companions were people of desires (أهواء) and opinions (آراء), which are matters prone to disagreement. This is contrary to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and His followers (شيعته), for they were wholly devoted (سلماً) to Allah and His Messenger, and they were people of a clear designation (نصّ) from Allah and His Messenger, concerning which there is no disagreement. Therefore, the companions of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) believed Him to be the one whose obedience is obligatory (مفترض الطّاعة), unlike the companions of Abu Bakr.

هَلْ يَسْتَوِيانِ مَثَلاً الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ** (29) Are the two comparable? All praise belongs to Allah! No one besides Him shares in it, because He is the Benefactor (المنعم) by His Essence (بالذّات). بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** (But most of them do not know.) so they associate others with Him due to their extreme ignorance (لفرط جهلهم).

** إِنَّكَ مَيِّتٌ وَإِنَّهُمْ مَيِّتُونَ ** (30) You will surely die, and they too will surely die. For all are on the verge of death. ** ثُمَّ إِنَّكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ عِنْدَ رَبِّكُمْ تَخْتَصِمُونَ. (31) Then on the Day of Resurrection you will dispute before your Lord. Al-Qummi said: It means the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and those who usurped His right. ** فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنْ كَذَبَ عَلَى اللهِ وَكَذَّبَ بِالصِّدْقِ إِذْ جاءَهُ ** (32) So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who lies against Allah and denies the truth when it comes to him? He said: It means that which the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم brought of the truth (الحقّ) and the Wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). أَلَيْسَ فِي جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوىً ** (a) dwelling place (مقام) لِلْكافِرِينَ. Is there not in hell a lodging for the faithless?

(33) وَالَّذِي جاءَ بِالصِّدْقِ وَصَدَّقَ بِهِ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُتَّقُونَ. (33) And he who brings the truth, and he who confirms it—it is they who are the Godwary. In al-Majma', narrated from them (peace be upon them) and al-Qummi: He who brings the truth (جاءَ بِالصِّدْقِ) is Muhammad. And he who confirms it (وَصَدَّقَ بِهِ) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). (34) لَهُمْ ما يَشاؤُنَ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ ذلِكَ جَزاءُ الْمُحْسِنِينَ. (34) They shall have whatever they wish with their Lord. That is the reward of the virtuous. (35) يُكَفِّرَ اللهُ عَنْهُمْ أَسْوَأَ الَّذِي عَمِلُوا ** (35) so that Allah may absolve them of the worst of what they did, Let alone other (sins). وَيَجْزِيَهُمْ أَجْرَهُمْ بِأَحْسَنِ الَّذِي كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** and reward them their wage for the best of what they used to do. Thus, He counts for them the good aspects (محاسن) of their deeds according to the best of them, resulting in the increase and greatness of the reward (الأجر) due to the abundance (لفرط) of their sincerity (إخلاصهم) therein.

(٣٦) أَلَيْسَ اللهُ بِكافٍ عَبْدَهُ ** (36) Is not Allah sufficient for His servant? وقرئ عباده And it was read ʿibādahu (عباده) (His servants). وَيُخَوِّفُونَكَ بِالَّذِينَ مِنْ دُونِهِ ** And they frighten you with those besides Him. It was said: Quraysh said: "We fear that our deities will destroy you (تُخَبِّلُكَ) because of your disparaging them (لِعَيْبِكَ إِيَّاهَا)." And al-Qummi means: They say to you, O Muhammad, "Excuse us from ʿAli (peace be upon him)," and they frighten you that they will join the disbelievers. وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللهُ فَما لَهُ مِنْ هادٍ. And whomever Allah leads astray, there is no guide for him.

(٣٧) وَمَنْ يَهْدِ اللهُ فَما لَهُ مِنْ مُضِلٍ ** (37) And whomever Allah guides, there is none who can lead him astray. since there is no repeller of His action. أَلَيْسَ اللهُ بِعَزِيزٍ ** (Is not Allah an all-mighty,) Dominant, Unassailable (منيع), ذِي انْتِقامٍ ** (avenger?) He takes vengeance upon His enemies. (٣٨) وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ مَنْ خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ لَيَقُولُنَّ اللهُ ** (38) And if you ask them, 'Who created the heavens and the earth?' they will surely say, 'Allah.' due to the clarity of the proof regarding His uniqueness in creatorship (الخالقيّة). قُلْ أَفَرَأَيْتُمْ ما تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ إِنْ أَرادَنِيَ اللهُ بِضُرٍّ هَلْ هُنَّ كاشِفاتُ ضُرِّهِ ** (Say, 'Tell me, what about those whom you invoke besides Allah? Should Allah desire some distress for me, can they remove His distress?') Meaning: Have you considered, after you have ascertained that the Creator of the universe is Allah, whether your deities, if Allah intends to afflict me with harm, can remove it? أَوْ أَرادَنِي بِرَحْمَةٍ ** ('Or should He desire some mercy for me,) with benefit, هَلْ هُنَّ مُمْسِكاتُ رَحْمَتِهِ ** (can they withhold His mercy?') and thus withhold it from me? And it was read with the tanwin on both tā’s and the accusative case (نصب) for the two objects. قُلْ حَسْبِيَ اللهُ (Say, 'Allah is sufficient for me,') sufficient in bringing about good and removing harm. And it is narrated: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله asked them, and they remained silent, so [this Ayah] was revealed. And in the use of feminine pronouns [referring to the deities] according to how they describe them, there is an indication of their complete weakness. عَلَيْهِ يَتَوَكَّلُ الْمُتَوَكِّلُونَ ** (In Him let the trusting put their trust.') due to their knowledge that everything originates from Him.

(٣٩) قُلْ يا قَوْمِ اعْمَلُوا عَلى مَكانَتِكُمْ ** according to your state (على حالكم). And it was recited (وقرئ) مكاناتكم. إِنِّي عامِلٌ ** meaning, according to my station (على مكانتي). فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ. (Say, 'O my people! Act according to your station. I am acting [too]. Soon you will know) (٤٠) مَنْ يَأْتِيهِ عَذابٌ يُخْزِيهِ ** who is the vanquished one in both abodes (الدّارين), for the humiliation (خزي) of His enemies is proof of His victory, and Allah certainly humiliated them on the Day of Badr (يوم بَدر). وَيَحِلُّ عَلَيْهِ عَذابٌ مُقِيمٌ ** perpetual (دائم), which is the punishment of the Fire (عذاب النار). (whom a humiliating punishment will overtake and on whom a lasting punishment will descend.') (٤١) إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ لِلنَّاسِ ** for their benefits (لمصالحهم) in their worldly life (معاشهم) and their afterlife (معادهم). بِالْحَقِ ** being invested with it. فَمَنِ اهْتَدى فَلِنَفْسِهِ ** he benefits his own soul by it. وَمَنْ ضَلَّ فَإِنَّما يَضِلُّ عَلَيْها ** for its evil consequence (وباله) does not pass beyond it. ** وَمَا أَنْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ بِوَكِيلٍ ** so that you compel them to guidance (الهدى); rather, upon you is only the conveyance (البلاغ). (Indeed, We have sent down to you the Book for mankind with the truth. So whoever is guided, it is for his own benefit, and whoever goes astray, he only goes astray to its detriment. You are not a trustee over them.)

(٤٢)** اللهُ يَتَوَفَّى الْأَنْفُسَ حِينَ مَوْتِها وَالَّتِي لَمْ تَمُتْ فِي مَنامِها ** (42) Allah takes the souls at the time of their death, and those that have not died, in their sleep.

Meaning, He seizes them from the bodies by severing their attachment to them and their control (تصرّفها) within them, outwardly and inwardly, and that is at the time of death; or outwardly but not inwardly, and that is when they are asleep. فَيُمْسِكُ الَّتِي قَضى عَلَيْهَا الْمَوْتَ ** (Then He retains those on which He has decreed death), meaning He does not return them to the body, وَيُرْسِلُ الْأُخْرى ** (and sends the others back), meaning the sleeping one, back to its body upon waking, **إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ** (until a designated term), which is the appointed time for its death.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that he said: There is no one who sleeps except that his soul (نفسه) ascends to the heaven, and his spirit (روحه) remains in his body, and a connection (سبب) is established between them, like a ray of the sun. If Allah permits the seizing of the spirits (الأرواح), the spirit (الرّوح) responds to the soul (النّفس). And if Allah permits the return of the spirit (الرّوح), the soul (النّفس) responds to the spirit (الرّوح). And that is His saying, the Glorified: اللهُ يَتَوَفَّى الْأَنْفُسَ حِينَ مَوْتِها (Allah takes the souls at the time of their death) and the rest of the Ayah. So, whatever it sees in the dominion (ملكوت) of the heavens is something that has interpretation (تأويل), and whatever it sees between the heaven and the earth is something that the Devil (الشّيطان) makes it imagine (يخيّله) and has no interpretation. The explanation (الوجه) for reconciling the attribution of the taking of the soul (التوفّي) sometimes to Allah, and other times to the Angel of Death (ملك الموت), and yet other times to other angels (ملائكة أخر), has already passed in Surat al-Nisa. **إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ ** (Indeed, there are signs in that) upon the perfection of His power, His wisdom, and the comprehensiveness of His mercy, لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (for a people who reflect).

(٤٣) ** أَمِ اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ شُفَعاءَ قُلْ أَوَلَوْ كانُوا لا يَمْلِكُونَ شَيْئاً وَلا يَعْقِلُونَ ** (Have they taken intercessors besides Allah? Say, 'What, even if they do not control anything, nor are they intelligent?')

** أَمِ اتَّخَذُوا ** Rather, Quraysh took ** مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ شُفَعاءَ ** who intercede for them with Allah. ** قُلْ أَوَلَوْ كانُوا لا يَمْلِكُونَ شَيْئاً وَلا يَعْقِلُونَ ** Will they intercede even if they possess this characteristic, as you observe them?

(٤٤) قُلْ لِلَّهِ الشَّفاعَةُ جَمِيعاً ** (44) Say, 'All intercession belongs to Allah. No one intercedes except by His permission (إذنه). لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. No one possesses the power to speak concerning His command without His permission (إذنه) and His satisfaction (رضاه). ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ ** Then you will be brought back to Him.' on the Day of Resurrection (القيامة).

(٤٥)** وَإِذا ذُكِرَ اللهُ وَحْدَهُ ** (45) And when Allah is mentioned alone, ** اشْمَأَزَّتْ قُلُوبُ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ ** the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter shrink with aversion, ** وَإِذا ذُكِرَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ دُونِهِ إِذا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ ** but when those besides Him are mentioned, behold, they rejoice.

without their deities, they contracted and recoiled (انقبضت ونفرت). And when those besides Him are mentioned—it was said that it means the idols (الأوثان)—behold, they rejoice, due to the excessiveness of their infatuation with them and their forgetting the right of Allah, the Glorified.

Al-Qummi (said): It was revealed concerning فُلان and فُلان.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it (the verse) and He said: ** إِذا ذُكِرَ اللهُ وَحْدَهُ ** (When Allah is mentioned alone) means: by the obedience of those whose obedience Allah has commanded, from the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family). ** اشْمَأَزَّتْ قُلُوبُ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ وَإِذا ذُكِرَ الَّذِينَ ** (the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter shrink with aversion. And when those are mentioned) who are those whose obedience Allah has not commanded, ** إِذا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ. ** (behold, they rejoice.)

** قُلِ اللهُمَّ فاطِرَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ عالِمَ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ أَنْتَ تَحْكُمُ بَيْنَ عِبادِكَ فِي ما كانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ** (46) Say, 'O Allah, originator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the unseen and the manifest, You will judge between Your servants concerning that about which they used to differ.'

Thus, You alone are capable of judging between me and them, for I am bewildered by their disbelief and powerless regarding their obstinacy and the severity of their resistance (شَكِيمَتِهِمْ).

(٤٧) وَلَوْ أَنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ما فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعاً وَمِثْلَهُ مَعَهُ لَافْتَدَوْا بِهِ مِنْ سُوءِ **الْعَذابِ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** (47) If those who did wrong possessed all that is in the earth, and as much besides, they would surely offer it as a ransom from the terrible punishment on the Day of Resurrection.

(This is) a severe threat and a complete despair for them regarding salvation.

وَبَدا لَهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ ما لَمْ يَكُونُوا يَحْتَسِبُونَ And there will appear to them from Allah what they had not reckoned with.

(This is) an increase in emphasis concerning it, and it is similar to His saying: فَلا تَعْلَمُ نَفْسٌ ما أُخْفِيَ لَهُمْ (no soul knows what has been kept hidden for them) regarding the promise (of reward).

(٤٨) وَبَدا لَهُمْ سَيِّئاتُ ما كَسَبُوا وَحاقَ بِهِمْ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** (48) The evils of what they had earned became manifest to them, and they were besieged by what they used to ridicule. And its recompense encompassed them. (٤٩) فَإِذا مَسَّ الْإِنْسانَ ضُرٌّ دَعانا ثُمَّ إِذا خَوَّلْناهُ نِعْمَةً مِنَّا ** (49) When distress befalls man, he supplicates Us. Then, when We grant him a blessing from Us, We bestowed it upon him as a favor. قالَ إِنَّما أُوتِيتُهُ عَلى عِلْمٍ he says, 'I have been given it only because of a certain knowledge [I have].' based on knowledge from me regarding the ways of acquiring it, or based on [my knowledge] that I would be given it due to the entitlement I possess. Thus it was said. بَلْ هِيَ فِتْنَةٌ ** Rather, it is a test a trial for him, [to see] whether he is grateful or ungrateful وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** but most of them do not know. that.

(50) The Almighty's saying: ** قَدْ قالَهَا الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (Those who were before them have already said it.) meaning this statement, like Qarun and his people, for he said it and his people were pleased with it. فَما أَغْنى عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ ** (But what they used to earn did not avail them.) from the enjoyment (متاع) of the world.

(51) The Almighty's saying: ** فَأَصابَهُمْ سَيِّئاتُ ما كَسَبُوا وَالَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنْ هؤُلاءِ ** (So the evil consequences of what they had earned overtook them. And those of these [people] who are wrongdoers) the polytheists (المشركين) due to their transgression (العتّو), the evil consequences of what they earned will overtake them, just as it overtook those [before]. And indeed, it has already afflicted them with drought (القحط) and killing. وَما هُمْ بِمُعْجِزِينَ ** (and they will not be able to frustrate [Allah].) meaning, escaping (فائتين).

(٥٢) أَوَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ. (52) Do they not know that Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes, and tightens it? There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith. (٥٣) قُلْ يا عِبادِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (53) Say, 'O My servants who have committed excesses against their own souls—' They went to excess in committing offenses against them (their souls) by being extravagant in sins. لا تَقْنَطُوا مِنْ رَحْمَةِ اللهِ إِنَّ اللهَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ جَمِيعاً إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ ** 'do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed Allah forgives all sins. Indeed He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.' And al-Qummi said: It was revealed specifically concerning the Shi'ah of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Allah has indeed mentioned you in His Book when He says: يا عِبادِيَ ** (O My servants)—the Ayah." He said: "By Allah, He did not intend anyone other than you by this." And in al-Ma'ani and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "And concerning the Shi'ah of the progeny of Fatimah (peace be upon her), Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed this Ayah specifically." And in al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "No one is upon the religion (ملّة) of Ibrahim except you, and nothing is accepted except from you, and He does not forgive sins except for you." And from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He said: "There is no Ayah in the Quran more comprehensive than يا عِبادِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا ** (O My servants who have committed excesses)—the Ayah." And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), that he said: "I would not love to have the world and all that is in it instead of this Ayah."

(٥٤) وَأَنِيبُوا إِلى رَبِّكُمْ وَأَسْلِمُوا لَهُ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَكُمُ الْعَذابُ ثُمَّ لا تُنْصَرُونَ. (54) And return penitently to your Lord and submit to Him before the punishment overtakes you, whereafter you will not be helped. (٥٥) وَاتَّبِعُوا أَحْسَنَ ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَكُمُ الْعَذابُ بَغْتَةً وَأَنْتُمْ لا تَشْعُرُونَ ** (55) And follow the best of what has been sent down to you from your Lord, before the punishment overtakes you suddenly, while you are unaware. by its coming, so that you might remedy it. (٥٦) أَنْ تَقُولَ نَفْسٌ ** (56) Lest any soul should say, Out of dislike (lest) **أَنْ تَقُولَ يا حَسْرَتى عَلى ما فَرَّطْتُ ** (Lest it should say, 'Alas for me, for what I neglected) for what I fell short of فِي جَنْبِ اللهِ (in the side of Allah)—in His right, His obedience, and His proximity.

In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, the people who will feel the most regret (حسرة) on the Day of Resurrection are those who described justice (العدل) but then opposed it. And this is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: **أَنْ تَقُولَ نَفْسٌ ** (Lest any soul should say)—the Ayah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: جَنْبِ اللهِ ** (the side of Allah) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and likewise those who came after him from the Successors (الأوصياء) who hold the high station (المكان الرّفيع), until the matter reaches the last of them.

And in al-Ikmal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): We are جَنْبِ اللهِ. (the side of Allah).

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from him (al-Baqir), and from his father, and from his son (peace be upon them): This Ayah جَنْبِ اللهِ ** (the side of Allah) refers to Ali (peace be upon him), and He is the Proof (حجّة) of Allah over creation on the Day of Resurrection.

And narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: It refers to the guardianship (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him).

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): I am جَنْبِ اللهِ (the side of Allah). And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a tradition: And He, majestic is His remembrance, increased in clarification (التبيان) and the establishment of the Proof (الحجّة) by His saying concerning His chosen ones (أصفيائه) and His guardians (أوليائه): أَنْ تَقُولَ نَفْسٌ يا حَسْرَتى عَلى ما فَرَّطْتُ فِي جَنْبِ اللهِ ** (Lest any soul should say, 'Alas for me, for what I neglected in the side of Allah'), thereby making known to creation their proximity (قربهم). Do you not see that you say, "So-and-so is next to (الى جنب) so-and-so," when you intend to describe his proximity to him? Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, only placed these symbols (الرموز) in His Book—which none knows except Him, His Prophets, and His Proofs (حججه) on His earth—due to His knowledge of what the innovators (المبدّلون) would introduce into His Book by dropping the names of His Proofs from it and confusing the Nation (الأمّة) regarding that, so that they might assist them in their falsehood (باطلهم). So, He established the symbols (الرموز) therein and blinded their hearts and their eyes regarding what was incumbent upon them in abandoning them and abandoning other discourse (الخطاب) that indicates what they introduced into it: وَانْ كُنْتُ لِمِنَ السّاخِرينَ (and I was indeed among the mockers)—those who mock its people. Meaning: I neglected (my duty) while I was mocking.

(٥٧) أَوْ تَقُولَ لَوْ أَنَّ اللهَ هَدانِي ** (57) or lest it should say, 'Had Allah guided me, by guiding (me) to the truth (الحق), لَكُنْتُ مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ ** 'I would have been among the Godwary,' (meaning, those who avoid) polytheism (الشرك) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). (٥٨) أَوْ تَقُولَ حِينَ تَرَى الْعَذابَ لَوْ أَنَّ لِي كَرَّةً فَأَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** (58) Or lest it should say, when it sees the punishment, 'Had there been another chance for me, I would have been among the virtuous.' in belief (العقيدة) and action (العمل). This is even if it is only to indicate that he will not be free from these sayings, whether out of confusion (تحيّراً) or by offering excuses (تعلّلاً) that are futile (بما لا طائل تحته). (٥٩) بَلى قَدْ جاءَتْكَ آياتِي فَكَذَّبْتَ بِها وَاسْتَكْبَرْتَ وَكُنْتَ مِنَ الْكافِرِينَ ** (59) 'Yes, My signs certainly came to you, but you denied them, and acted arrogantly, and were among the faithless.' This is a rebuttal from Allah against him (the soul) due to the meaning of negation (النفي) contained within his saying: لَوْ أَنَّ اللهَ هَدانِي ** ('Had Allah guided me'). Al-Qummi means by the signs (الآيات) the Imams (peace be upon them).

(٦٠) وَيَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ تَرَى الَّذِينَ كَذَبُوا عَلَى اللهِ وُجُوهُهُمْ مُسْوَدَّةٌ. (60) And on the Day of Resurrection you will see those who lied against Allah with their faces blackened. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse. He said: "It refers to whoever claims that he is an Imam while he is not an Imam." It was asked: "Even if he is an ‘Alawi (علويّاً) and a Fatimi (فاطميّاً)?" He said: "Even if he is an ‘Alawi (علويّاً) and a Fatimi (فاطميّاً)." In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi: The like of it (is narrated). أَلَيْسَ فِي جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوىً ** (Is there not in hell a lodging place) — a station (مقام) — لِلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ ** (for the arrogant ones) — those arrogant against faith (الإِيمان) and obedience (الطاعة). Al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him). He said: Indeed, فِي جَهَنَّمَ ** (in hell) there is a valley لِلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ ** (for the arrogant ones) called Saqar (سقر). It complained to Allah about the intensity of its heat and asked Him to breathe, so He permitted it, and it breathed, thus burning Hell (جهنّم).

(٦١) وَيُنَجِّي اللهُ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا بِمَفازَتِهِمْ ** (61) And Allah will deliver those who are Godwary, by virtue of their success. by their success (بفلاحهم). And it was read in the plural. لا يَمَسُّهُمُ السُّوءُ وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ. (61) no evil will touch them, nor will they grieve. (٦٢) اللهُ خالِقُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَكِيلٌ ** (62) Allah is the creator of all things, and He is the guardian over all things. He undertakes the management (التّصرف) of it. (٦٣) لَهُ مَقالِيدُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (63) To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth. Its keys (مفاتيحها). No one besides Him possesses their affair or is able to manage them. And this is a metaphor (كناية) for His power and His preservation (حفظه) of them. وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآياتِ اللهِ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ. (63) And those who defy the signs of Allah—it is they who are the losers. (٦٤) قُلْ أَفَغَيْرَ اللهِ تَأْمُرُونِّي أَعْبُدُ أَيُّهَا الْجاهِلُونَ. (64) Say, 'Do you bid me worship someone other than Allah, O ignorant ones?' In al-Jawami', it is narrated: They said, "Touch some of our deities, and we will believe in your God." So, [this verse] was revealed. (٦٥) وَلَقَدْ أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ وَإِلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ ** (65) Certainly, it has been revealed to you and to those who were before you: from the Messengers (الرسل). لَئِنْ أَشْرَكْتَ لَيَحْبَطَنَّ عَمَلُكَ وَلَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ. (65) 'If you ascribe any partners to Allah, your work shall certainly fail and you will surely be among the losers.' (٦٦) بَلِ اللهَ فَاعْبُدْ ** (66) Rather, worship Allah, A rejection of what they commanded him to do. وَكُنْ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ ** (66) and be among the grateful. for His favor (إنعامه) upon you. Al-Qummi (said): This address is directed to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the meaning is for his community (أمّته). This is what al-Sadiq عليه السلام said: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with the principle: 'I mean you, so listen, O neighbor (إيّاك أعني واسمعي يا جارة).'" The proof (الدّليل) for this is His saying, the Almighty: بَلِ اللهَ فَاعْبُدْ وَكُنْ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ (Rather, worship Allah, and be among the grateful). He certainly knew that His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم worships Him and is grateful to Him, but He made His Prophet His servant through calling to Him, as a means of disciplining (تأديباً) his community. And from al-Baqir عليه السلام: He was asked about this verse, and he said: "Its interpretation (تفسيرها) is: 'If you command the wilayah (ولاية) of anyone alongside the wilayah of Ali عليه السلام after you, لَيَحْبَطَنَّ عَمَلُكَ وَلَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ. (your work shall certainly fail and you will surely be among the losers).'" And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "It means if you associate anyone other than Him in the wilayah (الولاية)." He said: بَلِ اللهَ فَاعْبُدْ وَكُنْ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ (Rather, worship Allah, and be among the grateful), meaning: بَلِ اللهَ فَاعْبُدْ ** by obedience (بالطّاعة), وَكُنْ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ ** because I supported you with your brother and cousin.

(٦٧) وَما (١) قَدَرُوا اللهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ ** (67) And they did not measure Allah with His true measure. They did not measure His greatness within themselves with His true glorification, since they described Him with that which is unfitting for Him. In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in a sermon of His, when those who equate Him likened Him to the fragmented creation, limited in its attributes, possessing regions (الأقطار) and directions (النواحي) that differ in their layers (طبقاته), and since He, the Mighty and Majestic, is the Existent by His essence (بنفسه), not by His instrument (بأداته), it was negated that they measured Him with His true measure. So He said, purifying Himself from the partnership of rivals (الأنداد) and elevating Himself above the comparison of those who measure Him with limits (الحدود) among the disbelieving servants: وَما قَدَرُوا اللهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ (the Ayah). Therefore, whatever the Quran guides you to regarding His attribute, follow it, so that it may be a means (يتوسّل) between you and His knowledge. Complete it (وأتمّ به) and seek light from the illumination of His guidance, for it is a blessing and wisdom granted to you, so take what you have been granted and be among the grateful. And whatever Satan guides you to, which is not an obligation imposed upon you in the Quran, nor is its trace found in the Sunnah of the Messenger and the Imams of Guidance (peace be upon them), then entrust its knowledge to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, for that is the ultimate extent of Allah's right upon you. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah is not described. And how can He be described when He has said in His Book: وَما قَدَرُوا اللهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ? Thus, He is not described by any measure (بقدر) except that He is greater than that. And al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Khawarij. وَالْأَرْضُ جَمِيعاً قَبْضَتُهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ وَالسَّماواتُ مَطْوِيَّاتٌ بِيَمِينِهِ (67) The entire earth will be in His grasp on the Day of Resurrection, and the heavens rolled up in His right hand. This is an alert to His greatness and the insignificance of the mighty creations in which imaginations are bewildered, when compared to His power, and an indication that the destruction of the universe is the easiest thing for Him. Thus it was said. And al-qabḍah (القبضة) is the instance (المرّة) of grasping (القبض); it is used metaphorically (أطلقت) to mean the handful (القبضة), which is the amount grasped by the palm. In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): His grasp (قَبْضَتُهُ) means His dominion (ملكه); no one possesses it alongside Him. He said: Al-Yamin (اليمين) and al-Yad (اليد) mean power (القدرة) and strength (القوّة). rolled up in His right hand (مَطْوِيَّاتٌ بِيَمِينِهِ) means by His strength and His power. سُبْحانَهُ وَتَعالى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ** (He is Immaculate and Exalted above [anything] they associate [with Him]!).

(1) Meaning, they did not glorify Allah with His true glorification, since they worshipped other than Him and commanded His Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) to worship other than Him.

(٦٨) وَنُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ ** (68) And the Trumpet will be blown, (Meaning: the first time) فَصَعِقَ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will swoon dead, (They fell down dead) إِلَّا مَنْ شاءَ اللهُ. except whomever Allah wishes.

In al-Majma', it is narrated, attributed to the Prophet (مرفوعاً): They are Gabriel (جبرئيل), Michael (ميكائيل), Israfil (إسرافيل), and the Angel of Death (ملك الموت).

And in a narration: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله asked Gabriel (جبرئيل) concerning this Ayah, "Who are those whom Allah did not wish to cause to swoon dead?" He replied: "They are the martyrs (الشّهداء), girded with their swords around the Throne (العرش)." Then it will be blown in it another time (نفخة أخرى): **فَإِذا هُمْ قِيامٌ يَنْظُرُونَ ** (68) and behold, they will be standing, looking on. (Standing up from their graves, turning their gazes in response.)

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sajjad عليه السلام: He was asked about the two blasts (النفختين), how long is the interval between them? He said: "As long as Allah wills." It was said to him: "Then inform me, O son of the Messenger of Allah, how will it be blown in it?" He replied: "As for the first blast (النفخة الأولى), Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commands Israfil (إسرافيل), so he descends to the world, and with him is the Trumpet (الصّور). The Trumpet has one head and two ends (طرفان). The distance between the head of each end and the other is like the distance between the heaven and the earth. When the angels see Israfil descending to the world with the Trumpet, they say: 'Allah has permitted the death of the inhabitants of the earth and the death of the inhabitants of the heaven.' He said: Then Israfil descends to the enclosure (حظيرة) of Jerusalem (بيت المقدس), facing the Ka'bah (الكعبة). When the inhabitants of the earth see him, they say: 'Allah, the Most High, has permitted the death of the inhabitants of the earth.' So he blows a blast in it, and the sound emerges from the end that faces the earth, and no living soul (ذو روح) remains on earth except that it swoons dead and dies. And the sound emerges from the end that faces the heavens, and no living soul remains in the heavens except that it swoons dead and dies, except Israfil (إسرافيل)." He said: "Then Allah says to Israfil: 'O Israfil, die!' So Israfil dies. They remain in that state for as long as Allah wills. Then He commands the heavens, and they shake violently (فتمور), and He commands the mountains, and they move (فتسير). This is His saying, the Almighty: يَوْمَ تَمُورُ السَّماءُ مَوْراً وَتَسِيرُ الْجِبالُ سَيْراً ** (9) The day the heaven sways violently, and the mountains move away swiftly. (Meaning: it is spread out) and the earth will be changed to other than the earth (وتبدّل الأرض غير الأرض). (Meaning: to an earth upon which sins were not earned, exposed (بارزة), having neither mountains nor vegetation, just as He spread it out the first time. And He restores His Throne (عرشه) upon the water, just as it was the first time, independent in His Greatness and Power. He said: At that moment, the Compeller (الجبّار), blessed and exalted is He, calls out with a loud voice (جهوريّ) from His presence, which the regions (أقطار) of the heavens and the earths hear: Whose is the sovereignty today? No one answers Him. At that moment, the Compeller, the Mighty and Majestic, says, answering Himself: لِلَّهِ الْواحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ ** (16) It belongs to Allah, the One, the All-dominant! 'And I have dominated all creatures and caused them to die. Indeed, I am Allah, there is no god except Me, alone, I have no partner and no minister. I created My creation with My Hand, and I caused them to die by My Will, and I will bring them back to life by My Power.' He said: Then the Compeller blows another blast (نفخة أخرى) in the Trumpet, and the sound emerges from one of the two ends that faces the heavens, and no one remains in the heavens except that he lives and stands up as he was. And the bearers of the Throne (حملة العرش) return, and Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النّار) are brought forth, and the creatures are gathered for the Reckoning (للحساب)." The narrator said: "I saw Ali ibn al-Husayn عليهما السلام weeping intensely at that point."

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: When Allah intends to resurrect the creation, He will send rain from the sky upon the earth for forty mornings, so the joints (الأوصال) will gather and the flesh (اللّحوم) will grow. He said: Gabriel (جبرئيل) came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, took his hand, and led him out to al-Baqi'. He brought him to a grave and called out to its occupant, saying: "Arise by the permission of Allah!" A man emerged from it, white-headed and white-bearded, wiping the dust from his head, saying: "All praise belongs to Allah, and Allah is the Greatest (الله أكبر)." Gabriel said: "Return by the permission of Allah, the Most High." Then he brought him to another grave and said: "Arise by the permission of Allah!" A man emerged from it, black-faced, saying: "O my regret! O my destruction (يا ثبوراه)!" Then Gabriel said to him: "Return to what you were in by the permission of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic." Then he said: "O Muhammad, thus will they be resurrected on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). The believers (المؤمنون) will say this saying, and those others will say what you see."

(٦٩) وَأَشْرَقَتِ الْأَرْضُ بِنُورِ رَبِّها ** (69) And the earth will glow with the light of its Lord. It was said: [It glows] with the justice He establishes therein. He named it light because He adorns the lands (البقاع) with it and makes the rights manifest, just as He named oppression darkness. Thus, in the Hadith: Oppression (الظلم) is darknesses (ظلمات) on the Day of Resurrection. And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: The Lord (ربّ) of the earth is the Imam (إمام) of the earth. It was asked: "So when he emerges, what will happen?" He said: "Then the people will be independent of the light of the sun and the light of the moon, and they will suffice themselves with the light of the Imam (peace be upon him)." And in Irshad al-Mufid, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: When our Qa'im (قائمنا) rises, أَشْرَقَتِ الْأَرْضُ بِنُورِ رَبِّها ** (And the earth will glow with the light of its Lord), and the servants will be independent of the light of the sun and the light of the moon, and the darkness will vanish. وَوُضِعَ الْكِتابُ ** (and the Book is set up) for the reckoning, وَجِيءَ بِالنَّبِيِّينَ وَالشُّهَداءِ (and the prophets and the witnesses are brought). Al-Qummi [said]: The witnesses (الشهداء) are the Imams (peace be upon them). And the proof for this is His Almighty saying in Surat al-Hajj: لِيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ شَهِيداً عَلَيْكُمْ وَتَكُونُوا ** (so that the Apostle may be a witness to you, and that you may be) — you, O company of Imams (يا معشر الأئمّة) — شُهَداءَ عَلَى النَّاسِوَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ ** (witnesses to mankind. And judgment is made between them) — between the servants (العباد*) — بِالْحَقِّ وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ.** (with justice, and they will not be wronged.)

(٧٠) وَوُفِّيَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ما عَمِلَتْ its recompense وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِما يَفْعَلُونَ Thus, nothing of their deeds escapes Him. (70) And every soul shall be paid in full for what it has done, and He is the best knower of what they do. (٧١) وَسِيقَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِلى جَهَنَّمَ زُمَراً separate groups (أفواجاً متفرّقة), some following others, according to the variation in their degrees (أقدامهم) of misguidance (الضلالة) and wickedness (الشرارة) حَتَّى إِذا جاؤُها فُتِحَتْ أَبْوابُها so that they may enter it. And it was read with the lightening (تخفيف) of the tā' (التاء). وَقالَ لَهُمْ خَزَنَتُها in rebuke (تقريعاً) and reproach (توبيخاً): أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِنْكُمْ from your own kind (جنسكم) يَتْلُونَ عَلَيْكُمْ آياتِ رَبِّكُمْ وَيُنْذِرُونَكُمْ لِقاءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هذا قالُوا بَلى وَلكِنْ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَةُ الْعَذابِ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ (meaning) the word (كلمة) of Allah concerning the punishment upon us, which is His decree (الحكم) upon them of wretchedness (الشّقاوة) and that they are among the inhabitants of the Fire (أهل النار). (71) The faithless will be driven to hell in throngs. When they reach it, its gates will be opened. Its keepers will say to them, 'Did not apostles from among yourselves come to you, reciting to you the signs of your Lord, and warning you of the encounter of this day of yours?' They will say, 'Yes, but the sentence of the punishment became due for the faithless.'

** قِيلَ ادْخُلُوا أَبْوابَ جَهَنَّمَ خالِدِينَ فِيها فَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى الْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ ** (72) It will be said, 'Enter the gates of hell, to remain in it forever.' Evil is the abode of the arrogant. The information explaining the gates of Hell (جهنّم) has already passed in Surat al-Hijr. ** وَسِيقَ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا رَبَّهُمْ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ ** (73) Those who were wary of their Lord will be driven toward paradise [This driving is] a swift movement for them toward the Abode of Honor (دار الكرامة), and they will be driven while riding, as passed in Surat Maryam (peace be upon her). زُمَراً ** in companies. according to the difference in their ranks in honor (الشّرف) and the loftiness of their station (الطبقة). حَتَّى إِذا جاؤُها وَفُتِحَتْ أَبْوابُها ** When they reach it, and its gates have been opened, It is said that the answer (جواب) to idha (إذا) [when] is omitted to indicate that they will receive such honor (الكرامة) and veneration (التعظيم) at that time that description cannot encompass it, and that the gates of Paradise (الجنّة) will be opened for them before their arrival, awaiting them. وَقالَ لَهُمْ خَزَنَتُها سَلامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ** its keepers will say to them, 'Peace be upon you! No unpleasantness (مكروه) shall afflict you hereafter. طِبْتُمْ You have been purified (طهّرتم) from the filth of sins (المعاصي). Al-Qummi [said]: Meaning, your births (مواليدكم) were pure, because none shall enter Paradise (الجنّة) except one whose birth (المولد) is pure. فَادْخُلُوها خالِدِينَ** (Enter it to remain [in it] forever).

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq, from his father, from his grandfather, from Ali (peace be upon them all), he said: "Indeed, Paradise (الجنّة) has eight gates. A gate through which the Prophets (النبيّون) and the Truthful (الصدّيقون) enter. A gate through which the Martyrs (الشهداء) and the Righteous (الصالحون) enter. And five gates through which our followers (شيعتنا) and our lovers (محبّونا) enter. I shall not cease standing upon the Bridge (الصراط), supplicating and saying: 'O Lord, grant safety to my followers (شيعتي), my lovers (محبّي), my helpers (أنصاري), my allies (أوليائي), and those who took me as their guardian (تولّاني) in the abode of this world (دار الدنيا).' Then, a call will come from the depths of the Throne (بطنان العرش): 'Your supplication has been answered, and your intercession (شفّعت) for your followers (شيعتك) has been accepted.' And every man among my followers (شيعتي), and those who took me as their guardian (تولّاني), helped me (نصرني), and fought those who fought me, whether by deed or word, shall intercede for seventy thousand of his neighbors and relatives. And a gate through which the rest of the Muslims (سائر المسلمين) enter—those who bear witness that there is no god but Allah (لا إِله إلّا الله) and who did not harbor in their hearts the weight of a speck of hatred (بُغضنا) for us, the People of the House (أهل البيت)."

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Have a good opinion (أحسنوا الظنّ) of Allah, and know that Paradise (الجنّة) has eight gates, the width (عرض) of each gate is the distance of a four-hundred-year journey (مسيرة أربعمائة سنة)."

(٧٤) ** وَقالُوا الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي صَدَقَنا وَعْدَهُ ** (And they will say, 'All praise belongs to Allah, who has made good His promise to us,) concerning the Resurrection and the reward وَأَوْرَثَنَا الْأَرْضَ. (and made us heirs to the earth,) Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means the land of Paradise. **نَتَبَوَّأُ مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ حَيْثُ نَشاءُ فَنِعْمَ أَجْرُ الْعامِلِينَ ** (to settle in paradise wherever we wish.' How excellent is the reward of the workers!) Paradise.

(٧٥) وَتَرَى الْمَلائِكَةَ حَافِّينَ surrounding مِنْ حَوْلِ الْعَرْشِ يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ ** (75) And you will see the angels surrounding the Throne, celebrating the praise of their Lord. Remembering Him by describing His Majesty (جلاله) and His Generosity (إكرامه), taking pleasure in Him. And in this is an indication that the ultimate degree of the exalted ones (العلّيّين) and their highest pleasures is immersion (الاستغراق) in the attributes of the Truth (صفات الحقّ). وَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْحَقِ between the creation وَقِيلَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** And judgment will be passed between them with justice, and it will be said, 'All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.' Meaning, for what He judged between us with justice. And the speakers are the believers.

( A Narrative Discussion )

In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah Az-Zumar lightly from his tongue, Allah grants him honor (شرف) in this world and the Hereafter, and makes him mighty without wealth or tribe (عشيرة), until whoever sees him fears him. His body is forbidden to the Fire, and a thousand cities are built for him in Paradise; in every city, a thousand palaces; in every palace, a hundred ḥūrāʾ. And for him, along with this: عَيْنانِ تَجْرِيانِ ** (two springs gushing forth) and عَيْنانِ نَضَّاخَتانِ ** (two gushing springs) and جَنَّتانِ مُدْهامَّتانِ ** (two gardens of deep green) and حُورٌ مَقْصُوراتٌ فِي الْخِيامِ ** (houris confined in pavilions) and ذَواتا أَفْنانٍ ** (possessing various kinds of foliage) and مِنْ كُلِّ فاكِهَةٍ زَوْجانِ.** (two pairs of every fruit).

And in Al-Majma', the like of it [is narrated], without the phrase "lightly from his tongue," and without His saying **ذَواتا أَفْنانٍ ** (possessing various kinds of foliage) until the end.
